docs.title: Soulwright
docs.text:
A Minecraft mod where life itself is forged from souls, stars, and fate.

In Soulwright, every creature carries a soul shaped by cosmic forces. These souls can be captured, studied, combined, and bound into constructs known as golems. But power always comes with risk—because not all souls want to be controlled.

docs.title: 🌙 The Core Idea
docs.text:
Everything in the mod revolves around three forces:

  • Souls – the essence of all living things
  • Attunement – Angelic or Devilish nature
  • Zodiac – celestial identity that shapes behaviour and power

From these, you build machines, perform rituals, and create living constructs that evolve with you.

docs.title: ⚖️ Light and Darkness
docs.text: Souls come in two fundamental attunements:

docs.title: ✨ Angel Souls
docs.text:
• Passive and protective
  • Stabilise rituals
  • Enhance defence and healing
  • Prefer order and balance

docs.title: 🔥 Devil Souls
docs.text:
• Aggressive and volatile
  • Increase power output
  • Enhance damage and speed
  • Risk instability in complex systems

Mixing opposing forces can lead to catastrophic failure—or something far worse.

docs.title: ⭐ The Zodiac System
docs.text:
Every soul is aligned to one of 12 Zodiacs, influencing behaviour, abilities, and compatibility.

Zodiacs determine: - Golem AI behaviour - Ritual outcomes - Breeding compatibility - Machine efficiency

Understanding celestial alignment is key to mastering the mod.

docs.title: 🧪 What You Can Do
docs.text:
Soulwright is built around progression through discovery and risk:

  • Capture wandering souls in unstable form
  • Stabilise and store them in Soul Jars
  • Breed new souls using celestial logic
  • Build golems with up to 3 souls
  • Automate soul processing with arcane machines
  • Perform rituals that reshape reality

docs.title: Souls
docs.text: The foundation of everything. Souls carry traits, power, and instability.

docs.title: Rituals
docs.text: Magical processes that transform souls and reality itself.

docs.title: Golems
docs.text: Living constructs powered by one or more souls with emergent behaviour.

docs.title: Machines
docs.text: Arcane multiblocks that automate soul processing and resource generation.

docs.title: Starlight
docs.text: Celestial energy harvested from the night sky and converted into Soul Powder.

docs.title: ⚠️ Danger & Instability
docs.text:
Power in Soulwright is never safe.

  • Improper soul combinations can create Cursed Spirits
  • Overloaded golems may become unstable
  • Failed rituals can explode or corrupt the surrounding area
  • Angel/Devil imbalance can destabilise entire systems

Every powerful decision carries consequences.

docs.title: 🌌 Progression Overview
docs.text:
1. First Night – Encounter Wandering Spirits
  2. Capture Souls – Learn to stabilise and store them
  3. First Golem – Bind a soul into a construct
  4. Rituals & Machines – Expand your workshop
  5. Multi-Soul Constructs – Push stability limits
  6. Boss Encounters – Claim rare celestial essences
  7. Endgame Power – Manipulate fate itself

docs.title: 📖 Documentation Sections
docs.text:
Use the sidebar to explore:

  • Items, tools, weapons, and armour
  • Rituals and multiblock structures
  • Machines and automation systems
  • Entities, golems, and spirits
  • Bosses and progression challenges
  • Mechanics and system explanations

docs.title: 🔮 Begin Your Journey
docs.text:
The night sky is not empty.

It is watching.

And every soul remembers where it came from.

docs.title: Lore
docs.text:
The world has always been filled with invisible forces, though few have ever understood them.

Every living creature possesses a Soul—an immortal fragment of cosmic energy that records its existence, personality, and purpose. While the body eventually returns to the earth, the soul is destined to continue its journey beyond the mortal world.

Most souls pass on peacefully.

Some do not.

docs.title: The Two Attunements
docs.text:
Every soul is naturally aligned with one of two primordial forces:

  • Angel — Souls attuned to harmony, preservation, and order.
  • Devil — Souls attuned to ambition, conflict, and change.

Neither Attunement is inherently good or evil.

An Angel soul may fiercely defend its territory, while a Devil soul may simply seek freedom above all else. Attunement influences a soul's instincts rather than its morality.

This balance exists throughout nature, and both forces are essential for the world to remain stable.

docs.title: The Celestial Wheel
docs.text:
Beyond their Attunement, every soul is marked by one of the Twelve Zodiac Constellations.

These celestial signs influence how a soul behaves, how it responds to magic, and how compatible it is with other souls.

Scholars believe the stars constantly weave new souls into existence, imprinting each with a Zodiac before it enters the world.

The Zodiac cannot normally be changed without powerful rituals.

docs.title: Wandering Spirits
docs.text:
Occasionally, a soul fails to complete its journey after death.

Instead, it lingers within the mortal realm as a Wandering Spirit.

These ethereal beings drift across the world carrying an exposed soul at their core.

Their appearance and behaviour depend entirely on the soul they carry.

Angel Spirits are peaceful, often avoiding confrontation unless threatened.

Devil Spirits are aggressive and will actively hunt intruders who wander too close.

Each Wandering Spirit represents a single soul that can eventually be captured by skilled Soulwrights.

docs.title: Unstable Souls
docs.text:
When a Wandering Spirit is defeated, its body disperses almost instantly.

The soul, no longer protected by its spiritual shell, becomes Unstable.

An Unstable Soul can survive only a few moments before collapsing under its own magical energy.

If left uncaptured, it detonates in a burst of soul energy, permanently destroying itself.

For this reason, Soulwrights always carry Soul Jars when hunting spirits.

docs.title: Soulwrights
docs.text:
Very few individuals possess the knowledge required to manipulate souls safely.

Those who do are known as Soulwrights.

Rather than controlling life itself, Soulwrights study the magical properties of souls, learning how to preserve, combine, breed, and bind them into powerful constructs.

Their workshops are filled with strange multiblock machines, celestial mirrors, ritual circles, and artificial servants powered by captured souls.

To outsiders, their work appears miraculous.

To a Soulwright, it is simply another branch of natural magic.

docs.title: Golems
docs.text:
A soul can be bound into an artificial body through the Golem Forge, giving rise to a Golem.

Unlike mindless automatons, every Golem possesses a distinct personality inherited from the soul within.

A Golem's:

  • Behaviour
  • Combat style
  • Abilities
  • Temperament

are all influenced by the soul bound inside it.

As Soulwrights become more experienced, they learn to bind multiple souls into a single construct, creating companions of incredible power.

However, combining incompatible souls risks catastrophic instability.

docs.title: Instability
docs.text:
Souls were never intended to share the same vessel.

When multiple souls are bound together, their Attunements and Zodiac influences begin competing for control.

Compatible souls coexist peacefully.

Incompatible souls slowly destabilise the construct.

If instability grows too great, the Golem loses its identity entirely.

The souls fuse into a twisted consciousness, transforming the construct into a violent Cursed Spirit that attacks everything nearby—including its creator.

Many ancient ruins are believed to house the remains of experiments that ended this way.

docs.title: Starlight
docs.text:
The stars are more than distant lights.

They are conduits of celestial energy that continuously shape the world's spiritual balance.

Using specially constructed Mirror Arrays, Soulwrights can focus this energy onto ordinary Redstone, transforming it into Soul Powder.

Soul Powder forms the foundation of nearly every advanced ritual, making access to the open night sky essential for any successful workshop.

During rare celestial events, the stars burn brighter, greatly increasing the amount of Soul Powder that can be collected.

docs.title: The Balance
docs.text:
Neither Angel nor Devil may dominate the world.

Too much Angelic influence leads to stagnation, where nothing changes.

Too much Devil influence brings unchecked chaos and destruction.

The world's spiritual health depends upon the delicate balance between these opposing forces.

Every soul captured, every ritual performed, and every Golem awakened shifts that balance ever so slightly.

As a Soulwright, your choices will determine not only the fate of your workshop, but perhaps the fate of the world itself.

docs.title: Souls
docs.text:
Souls are the central resource of the mod.

Almost every piece of progression—from creating Golems to performing rituals—is built around acquiring, understanding, and manipulating souls.

Every soul is unique, defined by three core attributes:

  • Attunement
  • Zodiac
  • Strength

Learning how these properties interact is the key to mastering Soulcraft.

docs.title: Obtaining Souls
docs.text:
Souls are obtained from Wandering Spirits found throughout the world.

When a Wandering Spirit is defeated, it releases an Unstable Soul.

This soul exists for only a few seconds before collapsing into a violent burst of soul energy.

To preserve it, the player must quickly capture it using a Soul Jar.

Failure to do so results in the permanent destruction of the soul.

docs.title: Soul Properties
docs.text: Every soul possesses three immutable characteristics.

docs.title: Attunement
docs.text: A soul is naturally aligned with one of two spiritual forces.

docs.title: Angel
docs.text:
Angel souls favour harmony, protection, and stability.

Creatures carrying Angel souls are generally passive and defensive.

Angelic souls are commonly used for:

  • Defensive Golems
  • Healing effects
  • Protective rituals
  • Stabilising magical constructs

docs.title: Devil
docs.text:
Devil souls embody conflict, ambition, and raw power.

Creatures carrying Devil souls are aggressive and far more dangerous.

Devil souls excel at:

  • Offensive Golems
  • Destructive rituals
  • High-risk magical processes
  • Powerful weapon enchantments

Neither Attunement is stronger than the other. They simply represent different approaches to Soulcraft.

docs.title: Zodiac
docs.text:
Every soul is attuned to one of the Twelve Celestial Constellations.

The Zodiac determines how a soul behaves once bound into a construct or used in a ritual.

The twelve Zodiac signs are:

Position Zodiac 1 Aries 2 Taurus 3 Gemini 4 Cancer 5 Leo 6 Virgo 7 Libra 8 Scorpio 9 Sagittarius 10 Capricorn 11 Aquarius 12 Pisces

Each Zodiac grants different behavioural traits and magical affinities.

For example:

  • Aries favours direct combat.
  • Taurus values endurance.
  • Gemini embraces adaptability.
  • Virgo excels at precision.
  • Scorpio specialises in poison and curses.
  • Pisces prefers agility and evasive tactics.

The Zodiac also determines compatibility during rituals such as Soul Breeding and multi-soul Golem creation.

docs.title: Strength
docs.text:
Every soul possesses a Strength between 1 and 5.

Strength represents the amount of spiritual energy contained within the soul.

Strength Rarity Description 1 Common Weak but plentiful. Ideal for early-game crafting. 2 Uncommon Slightly stronger with improved abilities. 3 Rare Balanced souls suitable for advanced Golems. 4 Epic Highly sought-after souls with exceptional power. 5 Legendary Extremely rare souls capable of powering the strongest constructs.

Strength affects the effectiveness of the soul but does not alter its personality or Zodiac.

docs.title: Soul Compatibility
docs.text:
Not every soul works well with another.

Compatibility depends on:

  • Attunement
  • Zodiac
  • Certain advanced ritual conditions

Compatible souls coexist peacefully within a construct.

Poorly matched souls gradually increase instability, making powerful creations far more difficult to maintain.

Understanding compatibility is one of the greatest challenges facing an experienced Soulwright.

docs.title: Storing Souls
docs.text:
Captured souls remain safely contained within Soul Jars until they are processed.

For larger collections, players can build a Soul Repository, a multiblock capable of safely storing and organising hundreds of souls.

Repositories can sort souls by:

  • Attunement
  • Zodiac
  • Strength

allowing players to quickly locate the soul required for a ritual or construct.

docs.title: Manipulating Souls
docs.text:
As players progress, they unlock rituals capable of altering and combining souls.

Examples include:

  • Breeding compatible souls to produce new Zodiac combinations.
  • Purifying corrupted souls.
  • Corrupting purified souls.
  • Binding souls into Golems.
  • Expanding a Golem's soul capacity.
  • Stabilising multi-soul constructs.

Most advanced rituals require large amounts of Soul Powder and specialised multiblock structures.

docs.title: Unstable Souls
docs.text:
Immediately after a Wandering Spirit is defeated, its soul becomes unstable.

Unstable Souls:

  • Hover above the ground.
  • Emit bright particles.
  • Grow increasingly volatile over time.
  • Explode if left uncaptured.

Higher-strength souls release significantly more energy when they destabilise, making them more dangerous to recover.

Quick reactions and careful preparation are essential when hunting powerful spirits.

docs.title: Soul Lifecycle
docs.text:
A soul passes through several stages during its existence.

flowchart TD
    A[Living Creature] --> B[Wandering Spirit]
    B --> C[Unstable Soul]

    C -->|Captured| D[Soul Jar]
    D --> E[Rituals / Storage / Golems]

    C -->|Ignored| F[Soul Explosion Lost Forever]

docs.title: The Importance of Souls
docs.text:
Souls are far more than a collectible resource.

They determine the strength of your Golems, influence the outcome of rituals, unlock advanced crafting recipes, and shape nearly every aspect of progression.

A successful Soulwright is not simply one who collects the most souls, but one who understands how to harness their unique properties to create powerful and stable constructs.
